title: Positive control: trend-edge reproducibility on XAU D1
source: Conversation 2026-05-27: user asked for empirical proof the model works on gold using a historically-documented edge.
why: Demonstrate the pipeline measures a known edge (time-series momentum, Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen 2012) correctly before trusting it to discover new ones. Phase 1 = positive control (primary trend edge concentrates in trend regimes, evaporates in chop); Phase 2 = meta adds value over baseline OOS.
value_rationale: Empirical credibility of the whole pipeline; cheap to run; gates trust in every downstream verdict.
scope: scripts/positive_control_trend.py: rolling baseline Sharpe vs trend-strength (approach C), per primary, full 2004-2026 history
Spearman corr + tercile verdict (PASS iff corr>0 and high-trend median > low-trend median)
tests/test_positive_control.py on rolling-Sharpe helper with synth fixture
Phase 2: run full pipeline, report meta_sharpe vs baseline_sharpe delta

history.reason: positive control for trend edge, approved in brainstorming
feat(validation): positive control for trend-edge reproducibility (B0061)
positive control built, ran, verdicts produced (ema_cross PASS / momentum_zscore INCONCLUSIVE), Phase 2 meta-vs-baseline documented, tests green, committed 03b857a
